item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
You never fail to make me laugh, Cimmerian
+1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: As others said, too silly to be a mainlist scp and not entertaining or interesting enough to be a -J.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Spoilers for 'Merica
 

There's more than one George Washington.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
The idea of Ben Franklin's penis being able to warp reality checks out, +1. I cannot stop wondering, though: does it include the balls, or is it just the shaft?

Also: wait a minute, George Washington is still alive as an immortal nuclear-powered cyborg. So who did the Chaos Insurgency resurrect in his place during the ritual?

item.title: WTF did I just read
item.content:encoded:
Just read a skip which ended with a resurrected Ben Franklin breaking into a vault to reclaim his severed, indestructible penis, which then blasted off into the stratosphere with Ben along for the ride somehow.

No words. Should have sent a poet.

+1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Oh, there’s a picture of Benjamin Franklin. Does that mean that he’s an SCP-Oh…oh no…

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: This honestly reads like a -J, and not a particularly exciting one. A wishing penis that can turn into a rocket engine isn't exactly worthy of a mainlist article.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Needs a mention of how all testing was carried out by Big-D-Class personnel.

Still, the article did manage to warm the cockles of my heart.

+1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I really don't see why this is a mainlister and not a -J. It actively tries to act ridiculous to catch a laugh. Like, a severed penis became a rocket that is ridden by Benjamin Franklin…and it's his penis. A magic penis that alters reality when you hold it. God sake, that just screams -J. Yes, I am aware some mainlist articles can have a bit of humor but this is too much absurdity.

The article and imagery is hilarious, I admit that, but without the jokes in the article it is just bland. There is no story and the whole Chaos Insurgency thing is just a punchline. The immersion is broken easily too.

Really, it's competently written and funny, but it's a no. Sorry.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Thanks for the feedback!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
This is terrible.

Also

an effort to complete a coup of the US Government.

That's not how those words work.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Downvote. The only good in this are the jokes in the comments.

If it was a -j, it wouldn't be quite enough, yet, but at least would be on the way. As a main lister, it is too silly without any payoff. The penis isn't by far the silliest part, by the way.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Another Baby Boom.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I….I don't know, man. The rocket part just doesn't sit well with me. I'll keep away from putting a vote in, sorry.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: New GoI: Dong-theif version of Dr. Wondertainment.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Knowing the historical shenanigans that ol' Benny F's pocket rocket got up to in yonder olden days, it makes sense that it would have been anomalous. That it turned into an actual rocket is… something.

Also, what is the deal with chopping off the dorks of political figures? First Napoleon, then Franklin. Good grief.

I'm going to go scrub my brain with steel wool now.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
"Benjamin Franklin's Rocket Penis" is going to be the name of my new Viking-metal-bluegrass-fusion Cat Stevens cover band.

(Can't help but imagine even the most grizzled veteran MTF agents getting a little weirded out by this.)

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Of course!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Is the mainlist name a play on "Dick" being both a euphemism for penis & a short name for Richard? Still perfect otherwise. If "yes", even better.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
On July 3rd 2017, the Chaos Insurgency completed a ritual to resurrect several of the United States' founding fathers in an effort to complete a coup of the US Government.

I mean, there's a lot of things in here I can't not upvote for, but this is the one I copy-pasted. This is gold.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: A cockfight?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: What if Benjamin Franklin's dick met Rasputin's dick?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Novoting.

I love the absurdity of the concept, I feel like you don't actually embrace it. It's a penis that lets you alter reality when you hold it. Later, Benjamin Franklin reclaims it and… turns into a rocket? It's absurd, but in a way that comes out of left field and dilutes the main idea. The CI subplot was also a good way to expand the article beyond the penis (feel free to quote me on that), but at the same time felt like a contrived way of justifying the SCP's existence and pad out the article.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Poor Richard was actually my font of choice when doing projects for History classes in high school.

I was kind of in a constant state of "pfffft" while reading this, and I like reading articles that are on the shorter side, so that's a +1 from me here.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Same opinion I had in regards to Anborough's special article, if anyone here still remembers that.

Edit: why do i still remember that article

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
You look at a world with a story about Benjamin Franklin's rocket penis and ask "Why?"

I look at a world without a story about Benjamin Franklin's rocket penis and ask "Why not?".

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: why

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Seconding.

I was a bit in doubt of my vote, but after this, it's definitely an upvote.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Ohhh, I get it, you talked about it like it was a penis. Welcome to funny.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: This is now the only acceptable way to provide and respond to feedback.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Why.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: It's not necessarily size that matters but what you do with it. Unfortunately I can't say that Benjamin Franklin's penis was very satisfying. There just wasn't that much to take in and it was over much too quickly. I think there are lots of options for you to pump it up and make it a more enjoyable experience for everyone.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: 7/10 18th century Parisian Courtesans agree.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: You bastard.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I want to take a moment to apologize for Benjamin Franklin's penis not being long enough. I like to think that even though it's a little short, it does a lot with what it has. I'm not sure if it will be expanded, but I understand this happens to lots of articles.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: That was exactly my thought. It's a generic reality bender which happens to be a funny object.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Dunno. Just feels like the severed penis was more important than the scip.

The anomaly seems "tacked on" if you'll excuse the expression. Kinda like you thought "Hmm a severed dick, but what's the anomaly? Ah who cares, it's a severed dick! I'll just handwave about wishes and shizzles."

Neutral vote.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I want to love this but the idea is underdeveloped. The concept is great, in a "so-goofy-it-just-might-work" kind of way, but you don't go anywhere with it; if this were anything other than the penis of a founding father, it would be incredibly dull.

Good concept, execution needs work. No vote.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Mrrr….

Part of me really likes this.

The writer in me says "There's not enough here."

At the end of the day, I don't think there's enough content here to justify the set up, and that's disappointing, considering you're talkin' about the Founding Father of Dad Dick.

-1 for now, I'd like to see this expanded.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
No thanks. I do like silly ideas described dead-pan, but you haven't got enough exposition and exploration to bring your seriousness balance back into the black. In addition, stating that all the Founding Fathers were anomalous was immersion-breaking and too throwaway given the hefty implications of that fact. And, you know, the stupid ending.

I want to see this idea reposted, but only if the word count is at least quadrupled. There are lots of fun, funny things you could do with this, if you actually spend some time on it.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
The idea of an anomalous penis was fine, but…

an anomalous rocket penis is over-the-top.

anyways, here's your reward!

+1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: wtf even is this. I think oddly enough you picked the only founding father this specific concept really works with. +1 you goddamn maniac

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: It's just ridiculous enough to work, I'd say.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
SCP-3313 is the severed penis of Benjamin Franklin

SCP-3313 began to generate thrust in a similar manner to that of a chemical rocket engine and both SCP-3313 and SCP-3313-1 escaped the site upwards.

I am okay with this, and I am not okay with that.

+1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I feel like this is a wasted opportunity with regards to the nether regions of the man who flirted and/or slept his way through England and France, and authored such works as Advice to a Friend on Choosing a Mistress and a scientific essay entitled Fart Proudly.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Reading this made me feel like I was either too high or not high enough. Definitely a +1.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Far too wacky for me, I'm afraid.

item.title: wut
item.content:encoded:
wut
wut
wut
wut
wut
wut
+1
wut
wut
wut

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Considering how many "Franklin in Paris" jokes I've made over the years, I kind of have to upvote this.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I am afraid the humour here is rather lost on me :/ and the tone feels more -J than mainlist. -1.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Instructions unclear, got Dick stuck in containment.

This is amazing though. +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I can't say I had high hopes for a scip that's literally the severed penis of Benjamin Franklin, but I think it's oddly funny that Ben Franklin's severed cock is used as a reality altering tool.

Abstaining from voting, at least currently

E: FUCK that I'm upvoting.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: You had me at the severed penis of Benjamin Franklin and then lost me when it turned into a rocket engine, which frankly is an unsatisfying conclusion to what could have been a darkly hilarious short-form espionage thriller.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Poor Richard.

Anyway, this is a coldpost but I don't care, it had to be done.

Image is public domain and source is here: https://commons.wikimedia.org/wiki/File:BenFranklinDuplessis.jpg
